data.name: FfmpegNotFound
data.value: Video recording requested but ffmpeg was not found (set VideoRecorderOptions.FfmpegPath or add ffmpeg to PATH). Recording skipped.
data.comment: Warning shown when ffmpeg cannot be located.

data.name: RecorderReady
data.value: Video recorder ready (ffmpeg: {0}, granularity: {1}, persist: {2}). {3}
data.comment: Informational message at session start. {0} ffmpeg path, {1} granularity, {2} persist mode, {3} a granularity-specific hint.

data.name: HintPerTest
data.value: Recording each test into its own video.
data.comment: Hint appended to the ready message in per-test mode.

data.name: HintPerSession
data.value: Recording the whole session into one video.
data.comment: Hint appended to the ready message in per-session mode.

data.name: FailedToStartRecording
data.value: Failed to start recording: {0}. Recording skipped.
data.comment: Warning when the ffmpeg process could not be started. {0} is the error message.

data.name: StopTimeoutKilled
data.value: ffmpeg did not stop within the timeout; killing the process. The recording may be incomplete.
data.comment: Warning when ffmpeg has to be killed because it did not stop gracefully.

data.name: NoUsableVideoProduced
data.value: Screen recording produced no usable video (ffmpeg exit code {0}). {1}
data.comment: Warning when recording yielded nothing. {0} exit code, {1} last ffmpeg error detail.

data.name: WindowCaptureFallback
data.value: Could not resolve a visible current-process window (tried the process main window, the foreground window, and the console window); capturing the full screen instead. This happens on headless runs and can happen with terminals whose visible window is not focused at record time.
data.comment: Informational message when window capture falls back to full screen.

data.name: WindowCaptureUnsupportedOs
data.value: Current-process window capture is only supported on Windows; capturing the full screen instead.
data.comment: Informational message on non-Windows when window capture is requested.

data.name: RecordingCapReached
data.value: Video recording reached its configured rolling-buffer limit ({0}); older footage is being pruned to bound disk usage, so clips for earlier tests may be unavailable.
data.comment: Warning when the rolling-buffer size/duration limit is hit and old footage is pruned. {0} describes the limit that was reached.

data.name: CapDurationDescription
data.value: duration {0}
data.comment: Describes a duration cap. {0} is the duration.

data.name: CapSizeDescription
data.value: size {0} MB
data.comment: Describes a size cap. {0} is the size in megabytes.

data.name: ArtifactSessionDisplayName
data.value: Screen recording
data.comment: Display name for the whole-session video artifact.

data.name: ArtifactSessionDescription
data.value: Video captured during the test run.
data.comment: Description for the whole-session video artifact.

data.name: ArtifactPerTestDisplayName
data.value: Screen recording — {0}
data.comment: Display name for a per-test video artifact. {0} is the test display name.

data.name: ArtifactPerTestDescription
data.value: Video captured for test '{0}'.
data.comment: Description for a per-test video artifact. {0} is the test display name.

data.name: OutcomePassed
data.value: Passed
data.comment: Test outcome shown in a chapter bookmark.

data.name: OutcomeFailed
data.value: Failed
data.comment: Test outcome shown in a chapter bookmark.

data.name: OutcomeError
data.value: Error
data.comment: Test outcome shown in a chapter bookmark.

data.name: OutcomeTimeout
data.value: Timeout
data.comment: Test outcome shown in a chapter bookmark.

data.name: OutcomeSkipped
data.value: Skipped
data.comment: Test outcome shown in a chapter bookmark.

data.name: ChapterTitleFormat
data.comment: Chapter bookmark title. {0} test display name, {1} outcome.

data.name: OptionDescriptionCaptureVideo
data.value: Record the screen during the test run. Optionally specify when to keep the video: 'on-failure' (default, keep only failing tests' footage) or 'always'.
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video option.

data.name: OptionDescriptionSource
data.value: What to capture: 'screen' (default, the full screen) or 'window' (only the current process window; Windows only, falls back to full screen elsewhere). Requires --capture-video.
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video-source option.

data.name: OptionDescriptionGranularity
data.value: How recordings are split: 'test' (default, one video per test) or 'session' (one chaptered video for the whole run). Requires --capture-video.
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video-granularity option.

data.name: OptionDescriptionArgs
data.value: Extra arguments passed to the underlying recorder (currently ffmpeg), as output/encoding options. Requires --capture-video. Because the value usually starts with '-', use the '=' delimiter so it is not parsed as a separate option, e.g. --capture-video-args="-vf scale=1280:-1".
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video-args option.

data.name: OptionDescriptionMaxDuration
data.value: Limit retained footage to roughly the last N seconds (a rolling buffer) to bound disk usage on long runs. Older footage that no running test needs is pruned. Requires --capture-video.
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video-max-duration option.

data.name: OptionDescriptionChapters
data.value: Whether the per-session video includes one chapter bookmark per test: 'on' (default) or 'off'. Only applies with --capture-video-granularity session. Requires --capture-video.
data.comment: Help text for the --capture-video-chapters option.

data.name: InvalidOptionValue
data.value: Invalid value '{0}' for --{1}. Valid values are {2}.
data.comment: Validation error for an invalid option value. {0} value, {1} option name, {2} the list of valid values.

data.name: InvalidOptionPositiveInteger
data.value: Invalid value '{0}' for --{1}. Expected a positive whole number of seconds.
data.comment: Validation error for a non-positive-integer option value. {0} value, {1} option name.

data.name: SubOptionsRequireEnable
data.value: The video recorder sub-options require --{0} to be specified.
data.comment: Validation error when a sub-option is used without the enable option. {0} is the enable option name.

data.name: ExtensionDisplayName
data.value: Video recorder
data.comment: Display name of the video recorder extension.

data.name: ExtensionDescription
data.value: Records the screen during a test run using ffmpeg.
data.comment: Description of the video recorder extension.

data.name: CommandLineProviderDescription
data.value: Command-line options for the video recorder extension.
data.comment: Description of the video recorder command-line options provider.
